metadata.description:
A model for Coq core and extensions. Based on the Coq 8.15 grammar:
  https://coq.github.io/doc/v8.15/refman/language/core/basic.html#essential-vocabulary
  Extended with Vernacular commands for complete .v file generation.

literal.string: A Coq application with an explicit universe/type annotation on the function
A Coq application: a normal application or an annotated application
A single argument of a Coq application: an identifier, a natural number, or a term
An Axiom declaration: `Axiom <name> : <type>.`
A Coq binder: a name, typed binder, let-binder, implicit binder, generalizing binder, or pattern
One scrutinee of a Coq match, with optional as-name and in-pattern clauses
A Coq cofix expression: a cofixpoint body with an optional qualifier
The body of one cofixpoint definition: an identifier, binders, an optional type, and a term
The qualifier following a Coq cofix expression: an in-term or a with clause
A with clause listing mutually corecursive cofixpoint bodies
A Coq comment (* ... *)
A constructor in an Inductive definition
A Definition or Let command: Definition name binders : type := term.
A complete Coq .v file
One equation of a Coq match expression: a list of pattern lists together with the result term
A Coq existential variable reference
The form of a Coq existential variable reference
A field identifier used in a qualified identifier
A Coq fix expression: a fixpoint declaration with an optional qualifier
An annotation on a Coq fix expression: a structural, well-founded, or measure annotation
A measure annotation on a Coq fix expression, used for well-founded recursion by measure
A well-founded-relation annotation on a Coq fix expression
A with clause listing mutually recursive fixpoint declarations
A single fixpoint declaration: an identifier, binders, an optional annotation and type, and a term
The qualifier following a Coq fix expression: an in-term or a with clause
A Fixpoint command for recursive definitions
A Coq forall (dependent product) term: binders together with a body type
A Coq forall or fun term
A Coq fun (lambda abstraction) term: binders together with a body term
Terms surrounded by `( ) introduce their free variables as explicit arguments
Terms surrounded by `{ } introduce their free variables as maximally inserted implicit arguments
Terms surrounded by `[ ] introduce them as non-maximally inserted implicit arguments
A Coq generalizing binder, whose free variables are introduced as explicit or implicit arguments
A Coq identifier
An identifier argument bound to a term, as used in existential-variable instantiation
Pattern match on boolean values
The first form, with curly braces, makes name a maximally inserted implicit argument
The second form, with square brackets, makes name a non-maximally inserted implicit argument.
In the context of a function definition, these forms specify that name is an implicit argument.
Qualification for Require/Import commands
A single body in an Inductive definition (supports mutual induction via 'with')
An Inductive or CoInductive definition with one or more mutually inductive bodies
A let-in definition
Some constructions allow the binding of a variable to value. This is called a 'let-binder'.
The bindings of a Coq let expression: a named binder or a destructuring pattern
A destructuring pattern in a Coq let expression, in one of three grammar variants
A let-destructuring binding a list of names, with an optional return-as clause
A let-destructuring binding a single pattern, with an optional return type
A let-destructuring binding a pair of patterns, with a return type
A named let-binder together with any additional binders
Local or Global qualifier for commands
A Coq match expression: case items, an optional return clause, and its equations
A Module ... End block
A Coq name: an optional identifier (absent for the wildcard name)
A non-negative arbitrary-precision integer
A natural-number argument bound to a term, as used in existential-variable instantiation
A Coq function application: a head term applied to one or more arguments
A Notation declaration
A Coq floating-point number literal
A single Coq term, either an explicit qualified identifier or a lower-precedence term
The binders of a Coq forall or fun term: a single typed group or a list of binders
A Coq pattern: an inner pattern with an optional type ascription
The lowest-precedence Coq pattern: a qualified identifier, applied pattern, wildcard, parenthesized patterns, or a literal
A Coq pattern together with an optional notation scope
The highest-precedence Coq pattern: an as-pattern, applied patterns, or a qualified identifier pattern
A Coq pattern bound to a name via an as-clause
A Coq pattern applied to one or more sub-patterns
A Coq pattern matching a qualified identifier applied to sub-patterns
A Coq primitive notation literal: a number or a string
A qualified identifier
A qualified identifier together with a pattern, used in existential-variable and match forms
A qualified identifier with an optional universe annotation
The body of a Record definition
A Record or Structure definition
A field in a Record definition
A Require Import/Export command
The return clause of a Coq match or let, with an optional as-name
The name of a Coq notation scope
A Section ... End block
A top-level sentence in a Coq document, optionally preceded by a comment
The content of a top-level sentence
The sort 𝖲𝖾𝗍 intends to be the type of small sets.
The sort 𝖯𝗋𝗈𝗉 intends to be the type of logical propositions.
The sort 𝖲𝖯𝗋𝗈𝗉 is like 𝖯𝗋𝗈𝗉 but the propositions in 𝖲𝖯𝗋𝗈𝗉 are known to have irrelevant proofs (all proofs are equal).
The types of types are called sorts.
A Coq string literal
A Coq term: the top-level term grammar production, covering all term forms
A Coq term at the lowest precedence level, covering atomic and bracketed forms
A Coq term with optional trailing projection or scope annotations
A Coq term at application precedence: an application or a single term
A Coq term with an optional trailing type cast
A Theorem/Lemma/Proposition with a proof term
The kind of theorem command
A Coq type, represented as a term
A group of Coq binder names sharing a single type annotation
A Coq type-cast expression: a term, its ascribed type, and the cast operator used
The expression term10 : type is a type cast expression. It enforces the type of term10 to be type.
term10 <: type specifies that the virtual machine will be used to type check that term10 has type type (see vm_compute).
term10 <<: type specifies that compilation to OCaml will be used to type check that term10 has type type (see native_compute).
The operator of a Coq type-cast expression: normal, vm_compute, or native_compute checking
A Coq typeclass constraint binder, with an optional name and a generalizing flag
A universe annotation: a list of universe levels
A Coq universe expression: a maximum of universe expressions, or a single universe expression
A Coq universe level: Set, Prop, Type, an ignored level, or a qualified identifier
The name component of a universe expression: a qualified identifier, Set, or Prop
A single universe expression: a universe name with an optional offset
